changes.value:
## What

When editing a color picker cell in the grid (e.g. tag color), committing the edit snaps the cursor to the upper-left cell (0,0) instead of staying on the edited cell.

**Root cause:** Same as the EntityRow click focus bug (01KNCS1V). `EntityRow` onClick calls `setFocus(entityMk)` which sets focus to the entity moniker (e.g. `tag:tag-1`). This is NOT a cell moniker (e.g. `tag:tag-1.color`), so `derivedCursor` in grid-view.tsx (line 119-122) can't find it in `cellMonikerMap` and returns null. The mount effect (lines 132-141) then sees `derivedCursor` is null and snaps focus to `firstCellMoniker` — cell (0,0).

The color picker commit triggers entity-field-changed → re-render → the stale entity-level focus moniker can't be resolved → cursor resets.

**Fix:** This will be resolved by fixing EntityRow onClick (card 01KNCS1V). Once `EntityRow` stops overwriting cell focus with entity focus, `derivedCursor` will always resolve correctly from the cell moniker and the cursor will stay put after edit commit.

Additionally, the mount effect (lines 132-141) should be hardened: only snap to first cell on true initial mount, not on every re-render where `derivedCursor` happens to be null. Use a ref to track whether initial focus has been set.

**Files to modify:**
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/data-table.tsx` — fix from card 01KNCS1V (remove `setFocus(entityMk)` from EntityRow onClick)
- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx` — guard the initial-focus effect with a `hasInitialFocusRef` to prevent re-snapping on re-renders

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Editing a color picker cell and committing keeps cursor on that cell
- [ ] Editing any cell type and committing keeps cursor on that cell
- [ ] First mount still focuses cell (0,0) when no cell has focus
- [ ] Switching to grid view from board view still focuses cell (0,0)

## Tests
- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && pnpm vitest run src/components/data-table.test.tsx` — all pass
- [ ] Manual: grid view on tags — click color cell, pick color, verify cursor stays
Fix grid cursor reset to (0,0) after committing color picker edit

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,18 +1,25 @@
 ## What

-When editing a color picker cell in the grid (e.g. tag color), committing the edit snaps the cursor to the upper-left cell (0,0) instead of staying on the edited cell.
-
-**Root cause:** Same as the EntityRow click focus bug (01KNCS1V). `EntityRow` onClick calls `setFocus(entityMk)` which sets focus to the entity moniker (e.g. `tag:tag-1`). This is NOT a cell moniker (e.g. `tag:tag-1.color`), so `derivedCursor` in grid-view.tsx (line 119-122) can't find it in `cellMonikerMap` and returns null. The mount effect (lines 132-141) then sees `derivedCursor` is null and snaps focus to `firstCellMoniker` — cell (0,0).
+When editing a color picker cell in the grid (e.g. tag color), committing the edit snaps the cursor to the upper-left cell (0,0) instead of staying on the edited cell. Likely pre-existing.

-The color picker commit triggers entity-field-changed → re-render → the stale entity-level focus moniker can't be resolved → cursor resets.
+**Probable root cause:** The initial-focus effect in grid-view.tsx (lines 132-141) fires whenever `derivedCursor` is null AND `firstCellMoniker` changes. After a color edit commits, entity-field-changed triggers re-render → entities array changes → `cellMonikers` rebuilds → `firstCellMoniker` changes reference → effect re-fires → snaps to (0,0).

-**Fix:** This will be resolved by fixing EntityRow onClick (card 01KNCS1V). Once `EntityRow` stops overwriting cell focus with entity focus, `derivedCursor` will always resolve correctly from the cell moniker and the cursor will stay put after edit commit.
+**Fix:** Guard the initial-focus effect with a ref so it only fires on true initial mount, not on every re-render where deps change.

-Additionally, the mount effect (lines 132-141) should be hardened: only snap to first cell on true initial mount, not on every re-render where `derivedCursor` happens to be null. Use a ref to track whether initial focus has been set.
+```tsx
+const hasInitialFocusRef = useRef(false);
+useEffect(() => {
+  if (!firstCellMoniker) return;
+  if (hasInitialFocusRef.current) return;
+  if (!derivedCursor) {
+    setFocus(firstCellMoniker);
+    hasInitialFocusRef.current = true;
+  }
+}, [firstCellMoniker, setFocus, derivedCursor]);
+```

 **Files to modify:**
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/data-table.tsx` — fix from card 01KNCS1V (remove `setFocus(entityMk)` from EntityRow onClick)
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx` — guard the initial-focus effect with a `hasInitialFocusRef` to prevent re-snapping on re-renders
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/grid-view.tsx` — guard initial-focus effect with `hasInitialFocusRef`

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] Editing a color picker cell and committing keeps cursor on that cell
@@ -21,5 +28,5 @@
 - [ ] Switching to grid view from board view still focuses cell (0,0)

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && pnpm vitest run src/components/data-table.test.tsx` — all pass
+- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && pnpm vitest run` — all pass
 - [ ] Manual: grid view on tags — click color cell, pick color, verify cursor stays
\ No newline at end of file
